title: crateva urbaniana
intro: Crateva urbaniana, also known as the bivalve tree or elephant apple, is a tropical plant species in the family Capparaceae, commonly found in the Western Ghats of India. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. The Crateva urbaniana plant is valued for its health benefits, which include lowering blood pressure, reducing fever, and treating various skin conditions. The plant's herbal preparations include making tea, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to treat various ailments, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine.
